reference: USACE-RMC Numerics; C# test values verified against R package 'truncnorm'

cases.name: sequential_setparameters_recovery
cases.note: Mirrors Test_ParameterValidity.cs's SpecializedUnivariateSettersTrackFinalState (TruncatedDistribution section): a valid instance's SetParameters(flat) call with min >= max goes invalid; a later valid SetParameters recovers and GetParameters echoes the restored tuple. NOTE: this case only exercises the min>=max bounds check, which the pre-2a0357a C++ already validated correctly too (it directly checked `min_ < max_` in validate_and_set() after mutating the real base) -- it does NOT discriminate the ported ValidateParametersCore delta (transactional validate-on-clone of the BASE parameters); see setparameters_invalid_base_parameters_recovery below for that. See fixtures/README.md's set_parameters note and truncated_distribution.hpp's v2.1.4 header note.

cases.name: setparameters_invalid_base_parameters_recovery
cases.note: Discriminates the ported delta specifically: SetParameters with an INVALID BASE parameter (ChiSquared dof=0) and otherwise-sane bounds. Empirically verified (standalone build of the pre-task-3 header, git rev 32f07f0) that the OLD C++ does NOT gracefully catch this -- it mutates the real base_dist_ to the invalid params, then update_cached_cdfs() calls base_dist_->cdf(min_) DIRECTLY on the now-invalid base BEFORE validate_and_set() ever runs; ChiSquared::cdf() itself throws when parameters_valid_ is false, so the OLD SetParameters call throws uncaught (std::invalid_argument 'ChiSquared: invalid parameters') instead of leaving parameters_valid()==false. The NEW validate_parameters_core validates the candidate base params on a scratch CLONE and checks candidate->parameters_valid() BEFORE ever calling candidate->cdf(...), so it returns false gracefully with no throw (also empirically confirmed against the current header) -- matching C#'s ValidateParametersCore, which likewise checks `_baseDist.ValidateParameters(baseParameters, false)` before ever evaluating CDF on the candidate.
